data.title: Hick’s Law
data.description: Simplifies user choices to reduce decision time and enhance navigation.
data.technical_spec: Limits the number of visible choices in navigation and settings.

data.title: Fitts’ Law
data.description: Strategically optimizes button size and placement for ease of access and interaction.
data.technical_spec: Prominent, easily accessible buttons for main actions (like, comment, post).

data.title: Miller’s Law
data.description: Adheres to cognitive chunking to improve information processing.
data.technical_spec: Groups related content chunks to enhance user comprehension and retention.

data.title: Aesthetic-Usability Effect
data.description: Design focuses on aesthetic appeal which fosters a perception of ease-of-use.
data.technical_spec: High-quality images, smooth transitions, and rich content presentation.

data.title: Serial Position Effect
data.description: Designs prioritize important elements in strategic screen positions to improve recall.
data.technical_spec: Critical features and calls-to-action placed at the beginning or end of lists.
